signals.headline: US Regulators Officially Classified SOL and ETH as Commodities in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Regulators Officially Classified SOL and ETH as Commodities in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This classification eliminated major legal uncertainty for allocators, following spot ETF approvals (ETH in 2024, SOL in Oct 2025), enabling clearer institutional flows amid April price gains (SOL +6.3% to $86.55, ETH +8.6% to $2,377).[1]

signals.headline: Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The upgrade reworks consensus to cut finality from 12s to <200ms, positioning SOL for high-frequency trading and real-time payments, potentially boosting consumer apps over ETH L1/L2 limits; SOL hit record 167M monthly holders by Apr 14.[1][3]

signals.headline: Ethereum Transactions Jumped 41% Weekly | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Transactions Jumped 41% Weekly | NO | MED confidence**  
ETH reclaimed activity leadership from SOL (which led in 2025), driven by L2 fee rebates (Base/Arbitrum) and tokenized treasuries settling on mainnet; no new confirmation, but supports ongoing ETF AUM lead ($48B vs SOL $4.8B).[1]
